data.content:
Here's the full analysis of legacy patterns and how to modernize:

---

### Legacy Patterns Found

| # | Legacy Pattern | Location | SDK-Style Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Non-SDK `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` + `xmlns` | Line 2 | Use `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | **Explicit imports** of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Lines 3, 46 | Remove — SDK handles these automatically |
| 3 | **Boilerplate PropertyGroup** (`Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic`) | Lines 4–15 | Remove — all are SDK defaults |
| 4 | **Explicit Debug/Release configurations** (`DebugSymbols`, `DebugType`, `Optimize`, `OutputPath`, `DefineConstants`, `ErrorReport`, `WarningLevel`) | Lines 16–32 | Remove — all are built-in SDK defaults |
| 5 | **Explicit framework `<Reference>` items** (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) | Lines 33–40 | Remove — implicit with target framework |
| 6 | **Explicit `<Compile>` items** for every `.cs` file | Lines 41–45 | Remove — SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs` |
| 7 | **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** with hand-written assembly attributes | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Delete — SDK auto-generates from project properties |
| 8 | **`TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2`** | Line 11 | Use `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` (or upgrade to `net8.0`) |

### Modernized Project File

All 47 lines reduce to **~10 lines**:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

The `AssemblyInfo.cs` metadata (`AssemblyTitle`, `AssemblyDescription`, `AssemblyCompany`, etc.) maps to MSBuild properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`) that the SDK uses to auto-generate the same attributes at build time.

Want me to apply these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have the full picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize them.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Non-SDK `<Project>` element** — uses `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and the `xmlns` attribute instead of `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports** — SDK-style handles these automatically
3. **Verbose PropertyGroup defaults** — `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` are all defaults in SDK-style


4. **Explicit Debug/Release configurations** — all the conditional settings like `DebugSymbols`, `DebugType`, `Optimize`, `OutputPath`, and `DefineConstants` are already baked into SDK defaults
5. **Manual framework assembly references** — `System`, `System.Core`, and similar are automatically available with the target framework
6. **Hardcoded `<Compile>` includes** — SDK-style automatically globs all `*.cs` files without needing explicit declarations
7. **Separate `AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style generates these attributes directly from project properties, eliminating the need for a separate file

The `TargetFrameworkVersion` needs to shift from `v4.7.2` to the `net472` moniker in `TargetFramework`, though upgrading to .NET 6 or 8 would be worth considering. Once I make these changes, the resulting project file becomes remarkably lean.
